unit_04 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_04

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 258 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Sentence Equivalence
Asserting a need to preserve the ______ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.Asserting a need to preserve the ______ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.
答案：B, F　adaptability; flexibility
hallmarksubscriberigid
hallmark: standard unit headword | subscribe: standard unit headword | rigid: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system—under which the government controls every aspect of construction—creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i)______ of the building market. Such a complete (ii)______ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public’s interest.The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system—under which the government controls every aspect of construction—creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i)______ of the building market. Such a complete (ii)______ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public’s interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　liberalization; abnegation
liberalizationwholesale
liberalization: standard unit headword | wholesale: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
The children’s ______ natures were in sharp contrast to the even-tempered dispositions of their parents.The children’s ______ natures were in sharp contrast to the even-tempered dispositions of their parents.
答案：A　mercurial
tempereven-temperedphlegmatic
temper: standard unit headword | even-tempered: standard unit headword | phlegmatic: standard unit headword
#4 Sentence Equivalence
Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less ______ texts.Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less ______ texts.
答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive
soberone-stop
sober: standard unit headword | one-stop: standard unit headword
#5 Text Completion
In the early twentieth century, rural women in the United States could feel caught between, on one hand, national myths upholding a "natural" lifestyle that by definition seemed to eschew science and technology, and, on the other hand, a desire to (i)______ the new, modern standards of mechanized household practice that (ii)______ their (iii)______ by aligning them more and more with city women and modern life generally.In the early twentieth century, rural women in the United States could feel caught between, on one hand, national myths upholding a "natural" lifestyle that by definition seemed to eschew science and technology, and, on the other hand, a desire to (i)______ the new, modern standards of mechanized household practice that (ii)______ their (iii)______ by aligning them more and more with city women and modern life generally.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　adopt; threatened to compromise _; distinctiveness
eschewmyth
eschew: standard unit headword | myth: standard unit headword
#6 TC DOUBLE
Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i)________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i)________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sanguine; propitious
artificialpropitious
artificial: standard unit headword | propitious: standard unit headword

Publishers, publicists, and broadcasters love anniversaries, those occasions when historical events become (i)______ in (ii)______ culture of celebration. On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)______.Publishers, publicists, and broadcasters love anniversaries, those occasions when historical events become (i)______ in (ii)______ culture of celebration. On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　marketable artifacts; a commercial; consumption

#8 Text Completion
Our contemporary preference for natural expression over artifice is (i)______ the traditional Western view that the skill with which you expressed a position corresponded closely to the credibility of your argument. Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii)______. And “style” was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii)______ poor thought.Our contemporary preference for natural expression over artifice is (i)______ the traditional Western view that the skill with which you expressed a position corresponded closely to the credibility of your argument. Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii)______. And “style” was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii)______ poor thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a departure from; indifference; betrayed
elevatedameliorate
elevated: standard unit headword | ameliorate: standard unit headword
#9 TC DOUBLE
Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sporadic; ubiquitous
resilientubiquitous
resilient: standard unit headword | ubiquitous: standard unit headword
#10 Text Completion
Unable to escape their own literary tradition, literary critics either become the ______ of that tradition or, on the contrary, use their knowledge of it to reinterpret writers and trends from new perspectives.Unable to escape their own literary tradition, literary critics either become the ______ of that tradition or, on the contrary, use their knowledge of it to reinterpret writers and trends from new perspectives.
答案：B　guardians

Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic

#12 Text Completion
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
anthropogeniccontiguous
anthropogenic: standard unit headword | contiguous: standard unit headword
#13 TC TRIPLE
In the early twentieth century, there were so few meteorologists on college faculties that finding instructors was difficult. The (i) _________ of Harvard climatologist Robert DeCourcy Ward was that faculty attached to related science departments would take it upon themselves to learn enough meteorology to prepare and teach an elementary course. With their interest sufficiently (ii) __________ , students might (iii) _________ more advanced courses, thus opening the door for the establishment of meteorology curricula in colleges across the country.In the early twentieth century, there were so few meteorologists on college faculties that finding instructors was difficult. The (i) _________ of Harvard climatologist Robert DeCourcy Ward was that faculty attached to related science departments would take it upon themselves to learn enough meteorology to prepare and teach an elementary course. With their interest sufficiently (ii) __________ , students might (iii) _________ more advanced courses, thus opening the door for the establishment of meteorology curricula in colleges across the country.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　hope; piqued; demand
open
open: standard unit headword
#14 SE
The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are :while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are :while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.
答案：A, B　overblown; inflated
admittedly
admittedly: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　churlish; admire

#16 TC SINGLE
The museum exhibit perplexingly mixed significant cultural artifacts with gaudy trinkets, leading one critic to suggest that the exhibit's curator could not distinguish the important pieces from those that were __________.The museum exhibit perplexingly mixed significant cultural artifacts with gaudy trinkets, leading one critic to suggest that the exhibit's curator could not distinguish the important pieces from those that were __________.
答案：E　meretricious
mixed
mixed: standard unit headword
#17 SE
In early twentieth-century Peru, modernizing elites imagined the country’s mountain regions as teeming with indigenous peoples but thought of its Amazonian jungle as a vast, _______ expanse, a blank slate on which to inscribe modernity.In early twentieth-century Peru, modernizing elites imagined the country’s mountain regions as teeming with indigenous peoples but thought of its Amazonian jungle as a vast, _______ expanse, a blank slate on which to inscribe modernity.
答案：A, D　unpopulated; vacant
teem
teem: standard unit headword
#18 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
mandate
mandate: standard unit headword
#19 TC SINGLE
While there is nothing ____________ about the periodic disintegrations of the Chinese empire, its repeated resurrection within more or less the same boundaries clearly distinguishes China from other continental powers.While there is nothing ____________ about the periodic disintegrations of the Chinese empire, its repeated resurrection within more or less the same boundaries clearly distinguishes China from other continental powers.
答案：B　aberrant
aberrant
aberrant: standard unit headword

答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure
repertoire
repertoire: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sketchy; an exhaustive
manner
manner: standard unit headword
#22 SE
The fierce drought that is gripping the area-and imminent rationing and steep water price increases---is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.The fierce drought that is gripping the area-and imminent rationing and steep water price increases---is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.
答案：C, E　highlighting; underscoring

highlight: standard unit headword

Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　acuity; social and cultural context; outstrip

daunting: standard unit headword

答案：D　equanimity

答案：C, F　denounces; lambastes

答案：B, C　itinerants; peripatetics

Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder

Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
indebted
indebted: standard unit headword

#45 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring
jarring

答案：B, D　arrogant; hubristic

#47 Text Completion
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
pronounced
pronounced: standard unit headword
#48 Sentence Equivalence
There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them ______ to most readers.There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them ______ to most readers.
答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque
vapid

答案：D, F　fanciful; chimerical

#50 Text Completion
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
ancestor
ancestor: standard unit headword
#51 Sentence Equivalence
Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only ______ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only ______ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.
答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious
picky
picky: standard unit headword

Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　dispelled; friendly; costs

dispel: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　mockery; derision
stupefaction

Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report

implicit: standard unit headword

Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with
accede
accede: standard unit headword
#56 Sentence Equivalence
Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to ______ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.Since it was committed to the idea of science as an international, politically neutral enterprise, the Royal Society of London refused to ______ members from enemy nations during world wars of the twentieth century.
答案：B, D　expel; oust
oust
oust: standard unit headword
#57 Text Completion
Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
formulate
formulate: standard unit headword
#58 Text Completion
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

答案：A, F　accession to; acquiescence to

accession: standard unit headword
#60 Text Completion
Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile

If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.
答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude

ennui: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　a momentary; an evanescent

#63 Text Completion
The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift

The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. Blank 2

答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)______ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)______ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)______ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. 答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce

When the musician was fighting for the organization he heads to be taken seriously, his forceful pronouncements were regarded as (i)_______ by many in the jazz community. By now, however, his statements have taken on a different tone, becoming more (ii)_______, and consequently they are perceived as being more directed at coalition building.When the musician was fighting for the organization he heads to be taken seriously, his forceful pronouncements were regarded as (i)_______ by many in the jazz community. By now, however, his statements have taken on a different tone, becoming more (ii)_______, and consequently they are perceived as being more directed at coalition building.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　divisive; temperate
temperate
temperate: standard unit headword

Influenza is __________ virus: it is full of surprises and newness, prone to mutation and reassortment.Influenza is __________ virus: it is full of surprises and newness, prone to mutation and reassortment.
答案：E　a protean
ubiquitous
ubiquitous: standard unit headword

Some history books are pathbreaking. (i) ___________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) ____________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. Other works, valuable in their own way, fill in gaps by challenging readers to rethink issues that had been thought to be (iii) _____________.Some history books are pathbreaking. (i) ___________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) ____________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. Other works, valuable in their own way, fill in gaps by challenging readers to rethink issues that had been thought to be (iii) _____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　Revising; pursue; resolved
eschew
eschew: standard unit headword
#102 SE
When automobiles became ____________ in Italy in the early 1950s, the country's ancient towns were inundated by vehicles, and their historic piazzas became, for a time, little more than parking lots.When automobiles became ____________ in Italy in the early 1950s, the country's ancient towns were inundated by vehicles, and their historic piazzas became, for a time, little more than parking lots.
答案：B, E　pervasive; ubiquitous

A tendency of historians to focus on individual national groups in isolation may help explain this (ii) ___________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　ignore; oversight

highlight: standard unit headword

Even though no other species in the Rana genus of frogs exhibits such __________ habits, once crawfish frogs have finally been located, they are relatively easy to film.Even though no other species in the Rana genus of frogs exhibits such __________ habits, once crawfish frogs have finally been located, they are relatively easy to film.
答案：A　furtive

rigid: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) , she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) , she counseled caution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

sober: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　eschew; forswear

答案：D　hyperbolic

But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Blank 3

Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious

答案：C, F　trivial; inconsequential

Blank 2

O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. #189 Text Completion
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data
contradictory

Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. 答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk

But this predilection for (ii)______ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.An affinity for (i)______ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. #208 Text Completion
His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i)______ . He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.His new role gives the normally clownish actor a chance to impress audiences with his (i)______ . He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii)______ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii)______ manner he presents in this film.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　forbearance; madcap; reserved

manner: standard unit headword

答案：A　jarring

The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere effort

One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______ : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. (i)______ to propose measures that might (ii)______ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii)______ , they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　transient; relative; nonnegotiable
resilient
resilient: standard unit headword
#252 Text Completion
Barker’s account of how mores have evolved over time is illuminating. It reveals as (i)______ and (ii)______ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)______ , while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.Barker’s account of how mores have evolved over time is illuminating. It reveals as (i)______ and (ii)______ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)______ , while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　transient; relative; nonnegotiable

#253 Text Completion
Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)______ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)______ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.Hyana Kusiemko and her colleagues speculate that the (i)______ support among low-income workers for increases in the minimum wage is a form of last-place aversion: people who are in a marginally better position than the worst off seek to (ii)______ to distinguish themselves from those in last place.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　tepid; retain their ability

#254 Text Completion

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic

答案：B　scrutinized

#256 Text Completion
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

#257 Text Completion
Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　rich; problem; augmented

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic
